customer_anxiety_profile: Darla is passive and compliant — no expressed anxiety, no objections raised. Her engagement is low-key rather than resistant. The service-area check (unexplained to her) may have introduced mild uncertainty that was never fully resolved.
unresolved_concerns: Service-area boundary check: Holland confirmed internally that Darla qualifies, but never explained to Darla what was checked or why. If Darla asks the closer about this, the closer should be prepared to explain.

Roof age at 15-year boundary: Darla said 'about fifteen years.' Holland never probed for replacement plans. The closer should confirm actual roof age and whether replacement is planned before proceeding.
Credit ambiguity: Darla said 'around that' to the 670 threshold. This is not a confirmed qualification — the closer should run the actual credit check and not assume confirmation.
sensitive_topics_raised: Sole homeowner — no spouse or co-decision-maker on title. Appointment does not require coordination.
All-electric home with $500–$700/month BGE bill — high bill pain, high solar value potential.
exact_promises_rep_made: Holland promised to send a text message to Darla's phone with instructions for sending a bill photo.
Holland said 'we will keep in touch' — no specific time or date committed.
closer_should_know: The bill photo has not been received as of the end of this call — the appointment is contingent on Darla sending it. There is no locked time anchor on the follow-up. The closer (or a follow-up setter) should confirm bill receipt before treating this as a booked sit. When the bill is in hand: Darla is a 40-year homeowner, all-electric, $500–$700/month BGE, sole decision-maker — a strong qualifier once the bill and roof age are confirmed.
